description: Shared default mockResponses merged into every fixture. Individual fixtures override only the fields their scenario tests; everything else falls back to this canonical clean run so fixture files stay small and focused on the behaviour under test.

mockResponses.draft:
# Prediction Market Draft (canonical baseline)

## Refined Question
Will the referenced event occur by 2027-12-31?

## Window
Start: 2027-01-01T00:00:00Z
End:   2027-12-31T23:59:59Z

## Outcomes
1. Yes — the referenced event occurs before 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z UTC. Verified via https://example.com/feed.
2. No  — the referenced event does not occur before 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z UTC. Verified via https://example.com/feed.

## Full Resolution Rules
1. Source: https://example.com/feed (public, timestamped, mechanical).
2. Cutoff: 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z.
3. Threshold: 1 confirmed event.
4. Timezone: all timestamps are UTC.

## Edge Cases
1. Source unavailable at cutoff → defer 48h, then void.
2. Event postponed past cutoff → No wins.
3. Ambiguous reporting → resolver discretion with public log.

## Sources
- https://example.com/feed
mockResponses.updatedDraft:
# Prediction Market Draft (canonical baseline, updated)

This is the canonical updated draft. Content is identical to the initial draft — the harness always runs an update pass so the downstream claim/verify/route pipeline fires twice, but for clean fixtures the update is a no-op.

## Refined Question
Will the referenced event occur by 2027-12-31?

## Window
Start: 2027-01-01T00:00:00Z
End:   2027-12-31T23:59:59Z

2. No  — the referenced event does not occur before 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z UTC. Verified via https://example.com/feed.

## Full Resolution Rules
1. Source: https://example.com/feed.
2. Cutoff: 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z.
3. Threshold: 1 confirmed event.
4. Timezone: all timestamps are UTC.

## Edge Cases
1. Source unavailable at cutoff → defer 48h, then void.
2. Event postponed past cutoff → No wins.
3. Ambiguous reporting → resolver discretion with public log.

## Sources
- https://example.com/feed

claims.text: Will the referenced event occur by 2027-12-31?
Yes wins if the referenced event occurs before 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z UTC.
No wins if the referenced event does not occur before 2027-12-31T23:59:59Z UTC.

reviews.reviewProse: Baseline clean review: outcomes are MECE, source is public and objective, timestamps are UTC-anchored, no obvious manipulation surface.

rubricVotes.rationale: Yes and No partition the event space with an explicit void for source unavailability.
Single public URL source that is timestamped and mechanical.
All times are explicit ISO-8601 in UTC with a hard cutoff.
Source is a neutral third party with no stake in outcome.
Each resolution rule states exactly one proposition.
Source unavailable, postponement, and ambiguous reporting all have named resolutions.

judgeDecision.rationale: Baseline clean run; all reviewers agreed on every rubric item.

entailment.rationale: The refined question appears verbatim in the draft.
Yes win condition is in the outcomes block.
Feed URL and cutoff appear in outcome 1.
No win condition is in the outcomes block.
Feed URL and cutoff appear in outcome 2.
Feed URL is explicitly listed under Sources.
Threshold rule appears in Full Resolution Rules.

finalJson.refinedQuestion: Will the referenced event occur by 2027-12-31?

outcomes.name: No
outcomes.winCondition: Event does not occur before cutoff.

finalJson.shortDescription: Canonical clean test market for the eval harness.
finalJson.fullResolutionRules: 1. Check feed at cutoff. 2. Apply threshold of 1.
finalJson.edgeCases: 1. Source unavailable → defer 48h then void. 2. Postponement → No wins.
